---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

# ================================
# 1. Load libraries
# ================================

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)
```


#### CanadaEU2006X

```{r}
# 2. Import data - CanadaEU2006X

df <- read_csv("CanadaEU2006X.csv")
```
# 3. Clean data


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================


```{r}
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )
```

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================


```{r}
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")
```

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================


```{r}
p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Canada's Exports to EU, 2006",
    x = "GDP (Greece = 1)",
    y = "Canada's 2006 Exports (Greece = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)
```


#### CanadaEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("CanadaEU2006M.csv")
```

# ================================
# 3. Clean data
# ================================

# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================


```{r}
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )

```

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================


```{r}
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

```

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================


```{r}
p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Canada's Imports to EU, 2006",
    x = "GDP (Greece = 1)",
    y = "Canada's 2006 Imports (Greece = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)
```

#### AustriaEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("AustriaEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 3. Clean data
# ================================

# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Austria's exports to EU, 2006",
    x = "GDP (Greece = 1)",
    y = "Austria's 2006 Exports (Greece=1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(p3)

```

#### AustriaEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("AustriaEU2006M.csv")

# ================================
# 3. Clean data
# ================================

# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
p4 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Austria Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(p4)
```


#### ArgentinaEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("ArgentinaEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pAX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Argentina Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pAX)

```


#### ArgentinaEU2006M



```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("ArgentinaEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pAM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Argentina Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pAM)
```


#### AlgeriaEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("AlgeriaEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pALX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Algeria Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pALX)

```


#### AlgeriaEU2006M



```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("AlgeriaEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pALM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Algeria Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pALM)
```




#### SKoreaEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SKoreaEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSKX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "South Korea Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSKX)

```



#### SkoreaEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SKoreaEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSKM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "South Korea Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSKM)
```





#### NZealandEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("NZealandEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pNZX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "New Zealand Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pNZX)

```


#### NZealandEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("NZealandEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pNZM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "New Zealand Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pNZM)
```




#### SAEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SAEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSAX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Saudi Arabia Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSAX)

```

#### SAEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SAEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSAM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Saudi Arabia Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSAM)
```



#### BAEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("BAEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pBAX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Bangladesh Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pBAX)

```





#### BAEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("BAEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pBAM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Bangladesh Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pBAM)
```



#### SWEU2006X


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SWEU2006X.csv")


# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    exports_norm = exports / greece$exports
  )

# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(exports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")

# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSWX <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = exports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$exports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Switzerland Exports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Exports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSWX)

```



#### SWEU2006M


```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Import data
# ================================
df <- read_csv("SWEU2006M.csv")



# ================================
# 4. Normalize by Greece (GRC = 1)
# ================================
greece <- df %>% filter(country == "Greece")

if(nrow(greece) == 0){
  stop("Greece (GRC) not found in dataset!")
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_norm = gdp / greece$gdp,
    imports_norm = imports / greece$imports
  )


# ================================
# 5. Log-log regression
# ================================
model <- lm(log(imports_norm) ~ log(gdp_norm), data = df)

slope <- coef(model)[2]
r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared

cat("Slope:", slope, "\n")
cat("R-squared:", r2, "\n")


# ================================
# 6. Plot (exports)
# ================================
pSWM <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gdp_norm, y = imports_norm, label = country)) +
  
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df$gdp_norm),
           y = max(df$imports_norm),
           label = paste0("slope = ", round(slope, 2),
                          "\nfit = ", round(r2, 2)),
           hjust = 0, size = 3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Switzerland Imports to EU (2006)",
    x = "GDP (GRC = 1)",
    y = "Imports (GRC = 1)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

print(pSWM)
```
